channel.title: Tutorial on CrySPY
channel.description: Recent content in Tutorial on CrySPY

item.title: Restriction on interatomic distances
item.description: First, see Random Search (RS) for basic usage of CrySPY. You can restrict the interatomic distance in structure generation. Here is an example of [structure] section in the input file to limit minimum interatomic distance for a A-B binary system. [structure] natot = 8 atype = A B nat = 4 4 mindist_1 = 2.0 1.8 mindist_2 = 1.8 1.5 This means that minimum interatomic distances of A-A, A-B, and B-B are limited to 2.

item.title: Molecular crystal structure prediction
item.description: First, see Random Search (RS) for basic usage of CrySPY. In this section, we give a tutorial on the molecular structure generation part only. Since version 0.9.0, CrySPY has been able to generate random molecular crystal structures using PyXtal . You need to use a pre-defined molecular by PyXtal’s database (see, https://pyxtal.readthedocs.io/en/latest/Others.html#working-with-molecules ) or create molecule files that define molecular structures. Pre-defined molecule PyXtal currently supports C60, H2O, CH4, NH3, benzene, naphthalene, anthracene, tetracene, pentacene, coumarin, resorcinol, benzamide, aspirin, ddt, lindane, glycine, glucose, and ROY.
